item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Advanced Pattern Matching
item.description: Advanced Pattern Matching Planned: Advanced regex, semantic vs syntactic matching, cross-domain applications Related Forward References and Planned Knowledge Note Naming Conventions Anti-Patterns in Knowledge Management Emergence in Knowledge Graphs Graph Traversal Efficiency .

item.title: Anti-Patterns in Knowledge Management
item.description: Anti-Patterns in Knowledge Management Common mistakes that undermine a second brain’s effectiveness.

item.title: Atomic Note Principle
item.description: Atomic Note Principle A note must be atomic — it contains exactly one single idea.

item.title: CLAUDE
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Vault Purpose This vault develops and refines a universal “Seed” — a portable ruleset that lets an AI agent build a high-quality knowledge base on ANY topic from scratch.

item.title: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases
item.description: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases A knowledge base can be customized for any field. Here’s how to adapt for different domains.

item.title: Forward References and Planned Knowledge
item.description: Forward References and Planned Knowledge When building a knowledge base, you often need to reference concepts that don’t exist yet — future topics, planned projects, or ideas you’ll develop later.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - AI-Generated Content Verification
item.description: Frontier Exploration: AI-Generated Content Verification The Gap The Seed covers: Source citation requirements Confidence markers Procedural knowledge verification AI-optimized knowledge structures But there’s no explicit guidance on: How to verify that content generated by AI assistants is accurate ...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - AI-Optimized Knowledge Bases
item.description: Frontier Exploration: AI-Optimized Knowledge Bases How should knowledge bases be structured when the primary consumer is an AI agent rather than a human? The Problem Space The current Seed assumes human readers who can: Follow natural language explanations Ask clarifying questions when confused Infe...

item.title: Frontier Exploration - API and External Service Dependencies
item.description: Frontier Exploration - API and External Service Dependencies The Gap The Seed covers: Software version dependencies — which software version knowledge applies to (version:, eol-estimate) Physical equipment dependencies — what tools are required to execute knowledge But it does NOT cover: External se...
